summary: Ukrainian forces celebrated a 1,500‑day defense record at Mala Tokmachka, while Russian forces reported mixed results from recent missile and drone strikes, including a mid‑range Oreshnik missile hitting Bilіa Tserkva and another striking an area near Avdiivka. Both sides claim limited territorial gains on multiple fronts, with Russian assault units advancing up to 900 m on the Sumy front and Ukrainian UAV attacks disrupting power in Kherson. Civilian casualties continue to rise, notably 81 injured in a night raid on Kyiv.
key_points: Ukrainian 118th separate mechanized brigade entered the Ukrainian record book for defending Mala Tokmachka for over 1,500 days (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Two Oreshnik mid‑range ballistic missiles were launched; one struck Bilіa Tserkva in Ukraine, the other hit a Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka or Yasynuvata (Source: @eRadarrua/93606).
Russian assault units of the "North" Army Group advanced up to 900 m on twenty‑one sectors of the Sumy front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian UAV attacks caused complete power loss in nine districts of Kherson region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
A night Russian strike on Kyiv resulted in 81 injured, including three children, and damage to the Lukianivska metro station and a shopping centre (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Russian air defence repelled a massive Ukrainian UAV attack near Yaroslavl, with a civilian woman injured (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Drone attacks in Belgorod region injured civilians in Shebekino and killed a man in Grayvoron (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces marked a 1,500‑day defense record at Mala Tokmachka, while Russia reported mixed outcomes from recent missile and drone operations, including an Oreshnik strike on Bilіa Tserkva and another near Avdiivka. Both sides claim limited advances, with Russian units moving up to 900 m on the Sumy front and Ukrainian UAVs disrupting power in Kherson. Civilian casualties rose sharply after a night raid on Kyiv, highlighting ongoing high‑intensity conflict.

points: The 118th separate mechanized brigade was entered into the Ukrainian record book for maintaining control of the front line at Mala Tokmachka for more than 1,500 days, underscoring sustained defensive capability in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Ukrainian UAV operations caused a total blackout across nine districts of the Kherson region, indicating effective targeting of energy infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).

Ukrainian forces reported continued skirmishes and limited advances on the Sumy front, with assault units moving up to 900 m on twenty‑one sectors, reflecting incremental pressure on Russian positions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).

Ukrainian air defence successfully intercepted a large Ukrainian UAV swarm near Yaroslavl, preventing further damage, though a civilian woman was injured in the incident (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).

Two Oreshnik ballistic missiles were launched; one struck Bilіa Tserkva in Ukraine, while the second hit a Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka or Yasynuvata, with speculation it may have impacted Russian forces (Source: @eRadarrua/93606).
Russian air defence and electronic warfare forces repelled a massive Ukrainian UAV attack on the approach to Yaroslavl, demonstrating continued air‑defence activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Drone attacks in Belgorod region resulted in civilian injuries: an FPV drone injured a man in Shebekino and a separate drone strike killed a man in Grayvoron (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).

Russian assault units of the "North" Army Group reported advances of up to 1,100 m on the Volchansk sector of the Kharkiv front, indicating localized offensive momentum (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
On the Sumy front, Russian "North" Army Group units advanced up to 900 m on twenty‑one sectors, with ongoing skirmishes in Zapselye, Ivolzhansk, and Kondratovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
In the Dnipropetrovsk region, the "East" Army Group advanced northwest of Alexandrovgrad, engaging in forested combat near the Volchya River toward Lesnoye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian forces reported progress on the East‑Zaporozhye front near Vozdvyzhevka and Komsomolskoe, using aviation, artillery, and UAVs to break Ukrainian defenses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian UAV attacks caused power outages in nine Kherson districts, affecting logistics and command‑and‑control on the southern‑eastern axis (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian "North" Army Group advanced up to 1,100 m on the Volchansk sector of the Kharkiv front, with skirmishes in Karaichnoe and Okhrimovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Skirmishes continued on the Sumy front in villages such as Zapselye, Ivolzhansk, and Kondratovka, with Russian units holding positions after limited advances (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported near Yaroslavl, prompting Russian air‑defence response and a civilian injury (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian forces noted an unsuccessful Ukrainian counter‑attack across the Seversky Donets River at Izbitskoe (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
A night Russian missile strike on Kyiv injured 81 people, including three children, and damaged the Lukianivska metro station and a shopping centre (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Russian forces reported a strike on Pavlograd, damaging a multi‑storey building and wounding three men aged 18, 43, and 45 (Source: @UkraineNow/81443).
Ukrainian UAV attacks caused widespread power loss in Kherson, affecting nine districts and likely hampering Russian logistical operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian reports indicated missile attacks on Belgorod and its district, damaging energy infrastructure and causing civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian air defence repelled a massive Ukrainian UAV swarm near Yaroslavl, with a civilian woman injured during the engagement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian UAV operations caused complete power loss in nine Kherson districts, demonstrating effective aerial strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Russian forces employed aviation, artillery, and attack UAVs to support assaults on the East‑Zaporozhye front, aiding breakthroughs near Vozdvyzhevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Drone attacks in Belgorod region resulted in civilian injuries in Shebekino and a fatality in Grayvoron, highlighting ongoing low‑altitude air threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).

key_developments: Ukrainian 118th separate mechanized brigade entered the Ukrainian record book for defending Mala Tokmachka for over 1,500 days (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Two Oreshnik ballistic missiles were launched; one hit Bilіa Tserkva in Ukraine, the other struck a Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka or Yasynuvata (Source: @eRadarrua/93606).
Russian "North" Army Group advanced up to 900 m on twenty‑one sectors of the Sumy front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Ukrainian UAV attacks caused a total power outage in nine districts of Kherson region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
A night Russian strike on Kyiv injured 81 people, including three children, and damaged the Lukianivska metro station (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Russian air defence repelled a massive Ukrainian UAV attack near Yaroslavl, injuring a civilian woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
Drone attacks in Belgorod region injured civilians in Shebekino and killed a man in Grayvoron (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30404).
sections.threat_assessment: Both sides are sustaining high‑intensity operations across multiple fronts, with Ukraine leveraging UAVs to disrupt Russian logistics and power grids, while Russia continues to employ ballistic missiles and air‑defence systems to counter Ukrainian aerial threats. The mixed success of Oreshnik missile strikes suggests limited precision, but the potential for collateral damage remains. Civilian casualty rates are rising, particularly in urban centers like Kyiv, indicating an escalation in strike density and potential for broader humanitarian impact.
sections.regional_response: Local authorities in Kyiv are managing increased civilian injuries and infrastructure repairs, while Ukrainian border services report no significant troop movements near the Belarusian frontier, though they note heightened Russian pressure on Belarus. Russian regional commands emphasize defensive successes against UAV swarms, aiming to maintain control over contested border zones.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of targeting energy infrastructure in southern Ukraine, warranting enhanced air‑defence allocation. The dual Oreshnik missile launches highlight a need for improved missile detection and attribution capabilities, especially in contested Donetsk areas. Russian advances of under 1 km on the Sumy and Kharkiv fronts suggest limited but ongoing offensive pressure, requiring continued reconnaissance and artillery support for Ukrainian defensive lines.
